Boerhaave, Herman.bio: **Herman Boerhaave** (1668–1738) was a Dutch botanist, physician, and humanist, often called the founder of clinical teaching and of the modern academic hospital. As professor at Leiden he held simultaneous chairs in botany, medicine, and chemistry, and his systematic approach to plant classification — organizing the university's botanical garden by his own taxonomy — influenced a generation of naturalists, including Linnaeus.
